data.description:
Render pane titles (from the pane metadata layer in CMUX-11) as first-class UI chrome — a centered strip above the pane that communicates identity at a glance, parallel in contract to the existing surface title bar, and themed consistently with it.

DEPENDS ON: CMUX-11 (pane metadata & naming) Phases 1 and 2 must merge first so the view has data to read.

DESIGN DECISIONS (from scoping conversation):

1. Centered title text, mirroring the surface title bar contract.

2. Optional — only renders when pane.metadata.title is set. Unnamed panes look unchanged for operators who don't adopt the habit. (Distinct from surfaces, which render chrome even for unnamed surfaces.)

3. Expand chevron toggles between title-only and title+description, matching the existing surface title bar affordance.

4. Dismissible via right-click → Hide. Dismissal is ephemeral session state (paneTitleBarUserCollapsed, reset per restart, matching Tier 1's ephemeral-state decision for surfaces). The underlying pane.metadata.title is untouched. Clearing the title permanently is a separate action (cmux clear-metadata or inline rename to empty).

5. Right-click context menu:
   - Rename… — flips the strip into an inline TextField, populated with the current title, that writes back via pane.set_metadata on Return. Cancel on Escape or click-away. Gives operators a first-class in-app renaming path without the CLI.
   - Hide title bar — per (4).
   - Expand / Collapse — toggles chevron state.

6. Theming unification: consumes the same theme tokens as surface title bars via the in-flight c11mux theming plan. Both layers feel like one system.

7. Dual-title rendering: when both pane and active surface are named, render both — pane title on top, surface title directly below. The stack reads as a path from 'why this pane exists' to 'what is showing in it right now.' Each strip retains its own collapse/dismiss behavior.

8. Bonsplit stays unopinionated; c11mux SwiftUI layers render the chrome above the pane, not bonsplit itself.

PHASES (each a PR):

Phase 1 — PaneTitleBarView (visible strip, chevron, collapse/expand wired up, no menu yet). This is the first user-visible milestone.
Phase 2 — Context menu (Rename, Hide, Expand/Collapse) + inline rename with first-class operator UX.
Phase 3 — Theming token unification with the theming plan's output.

PLAN DOC: docs/c11mux-pane-title-bar-plan.md

RISKS:
- Typing latency: any SwiftUI view added to the pane host is hot-path risk. Mitigation: strict hot-path review in Phase 1; view is equatable and only re-evaluates when title metadata changes.
- Chrome proliferation: multi-surface panes stack pane title + surface titles + tabs. Mitigation: decisions 2 (optional) and 4 (dismissible) let operators trim to taste.
- Theming plan churn: Phase 3 depends on theming plan's final token shape. Mitigation: Phase 1 ships with ad-hoc parity to surface title bars; Phase 3 migrates to formal tokens when they land.
data.title: Pane title bar chrome & theming

data.note: CMUX-12 (pane title bar chrome) depends on CMUX-11 (pane metadata mechanism) Phases 1 and 2 — store and RPCs must exist before the view has data to read.

data.body: Orchestration started 2026-05-05. Operator direction: scope has changed a lot since this ticket was filed (pre-rebrand, pre-CMUX-11). Delegator is instructed to start with Phase 0 (scope alignment): survey current codebase reality, post a comment with sections A (what ticket says) / B (current reality) / C (recommended scope), drop status to needs_human, and wait for operator confirmation before proceeding to Plan. Orchestrator: surface:20 (workspace:3). Delegator: pane:23 / surface:28 in same workspace. Worktree: /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11-worktrees/cmux-12-pane-title-bar on branch cmux-12-pane-title-bar (up to date with origin/main). Parent repo (canonical Lattice store): /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11. Prompt: <worktree>/.lattice/prompts/cmux-12-delegator.md.

**Phase 0: scope alignment.** Survey complete; posting A/B/C per delegator instructions, then dropping to needs_human.

---

## Section A. What the ticket currently says

Render pane titles (from CMUX-11's metadata layer) as first-class chrome above the bonsplit pane, parallel to the surface title bar contract, themed consistently with it. Three-PR rollout:

- **Phase 1.** `PaneTitleBarView` visible strip, chevron, collapse/expand. No menu.
- **Phase 2.** Right-click context menu (Rename, Hide, Expand/Collapse) plus inline rename.
- **Phase 3.** Theming token unification with the in-flight theming plan.

Eight design decisions, of which the load-bearing ones are:

1. Centered title text.
2. Optional rendering: only when `pane.metadata.title` is set.
3. Expand chevron mirrors the surface title bar.
4. Right-click → Hide; ephemeral `paneTitleBarUserCollapsed` per session.
5. Right-click context menu with Rename, Hide, Expand/Collapse.
6. Theming unification via the c11mux theming plan.
7. Dual-title rendering: pane title on top, surface title directly below, when both are named.
8. Bonsplit stays unopinionated; c11mux SwiftUI layers render the chrome above the pane.

PLAN DOC referenced: `docs/c11mux-pane-title-bar-plan.md` (renamed `c11-` post-rebrand).

---

## Section B. Current reality in the codebase

**CMUX-11 fully shipped.** `Sources/PaneMetadataStore.swift` (338 lines) ships full SurfaceMetadataStore parity: JSON values, `description` reserved key already accepted, source precedence, 64 KiB cap, monotonic revision counter, autosave-fingerprint integration, persistence via SessionPaneLayoutSnapshot. Phase 1–4 all merged (#22, #25, #27). Pane RPCs `pane.set_metadata` / `.get` / `.clear` plus `--title` create-time seeding are live. The view has data to read.

**Pane title bar work has not started.** `grep -RIln "PaneTitleBar\|paneTitleBar"` over `Sources/` returns zero hits. No view, no state field, no socket method.

**The plan doc still exists, post-rebrand.** `docs/c11-pane-title-bar-plan.md` (199 lines) carries the same content as the ticket, just renamed in the C11-1 rebrand pass. The ticket's `c11mux-pane-title-bar-plan.md` reference is stale; the file is `c11-pane-title-bar-plan.md`.

**Surface title bar precedent (`Sources/SurfaceTitleBarView.swift`, 353 lines).** Theme-engine adopted (CMUX-9 M1b): reads `titleBar_background`, `titleBar_foreground`, `titleBar_foregroundSecondary`, `titleBar_borderBottom`, `titleBar_backgroundOpacity` via `ThemeManager.resolve`. Consumes `chromeTokens` from environment for sizing (C11-6). Has chevron toggle plus Markdown-subset description rendering. **Title is left-aligned**, not centered: `Spacer(minLength: 0)` pushes the chevron right and the title rides at leading. Mounted in `Sources/Panels/PanelContentView.swift:25` above the per-surface content view, keyed by `panelId`.

**Theme engine is in production, not in flight.** `Sources/Theme/` ships 18 files (ThemeManager, ResolvedThemeSnapshot, ThemeRoleRegistry, ThemedValueParser, etc.). `titleBar_*`, `tabBar_*`, `sidebar_*` roles are live. `docs/c11-theme-namespace-plan.md` is the namespace plan. CMUX-9 M1a+M1b foundations plus surface adoption are merged. Phase 3's "consume same tokens as surface title bars via in-flight theming plan" is now "consume the existing `titleBar_*` roles." No waiting for a plan to land.

**Bonsplit does NOT expose a pane header builder.** `vendor/bonsplit/Sources/Bonsplit/Public/BonsplitView.swift` has three init forms, all exposing only `content: (Tab, PaneID) -> View`, `emptyPane: (PaneID) -> View`, `trailingAccessory: (PaneID, Double) -> View`. There is no view slot above the pane's tab bar where c11 can mount chrome without modifying bonsplit. This contradicts Decision 8's promise of "c11 SwiftUI layers render the chrome above the pane, not bonsplit itself": the only c11-only mount points today are *inside* the per-surface content (below the bonsplit tab bar), not above the pane.

**Sibling work converging on the same chrome layer.** CMUX-10 went `in_progress` 2026-05-06T03:47Z (a few hours ago) and is rewriting flash with: default yellow `#F5C518`, unified envelope across pane content plus sidebar tab, `--persistent` mode plus `cancel-flash` socket method, `flash_state` metadata overlay, configurable Flash Duration setting. Recent commits in the same chrome territory: C11-6 `App Chrome UI Scale (#123)` (`chromeTokens` env value across sidebar / tab strip / title bar), C11-10 `Per-surface tab colors inside panes (#124)` (per-surface tab tinting in bonsplit), C11-25 `surface lifecycle perf (#125)`. The pane title bar lands into a busy chrome layer.

**Hot paths near the would-be mount point.** `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest` (`Sources/TerminalWindowPortal.swift:134`) is gated to pointer events only; keyboard events bail before any work. `TabItemView` (`Sources/ContentView.swift:10940`) is `Equatable + .equatable()` and explicitly forbids new `@EnvironmentObject` / `@ObservedObject` properties without updating `==`. `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh` runs on every keystroke. The sidebar already threads ~16 fields into `TabItemView.==` including `chromeTokens` and `themedBackgroundColor`. Adding pane-title state to that pipeline must follow the same equatable plus precomputed-let pattern.

**Localization seam exists.** `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings` has live `titlebar.chevron.expand`, `titlebar.chevron.collapse`, `titlebar.empty_title` keys for the surface bar. New `paneTitleBar.*` keys would follow the same pattern, with a six-locale translation pass per CLAUDE.md.

---

## Section C. Recommended scope for this delegation

**Recommendation: ship Phase 1 + Phase 2 in this PR, defer Phase 3 entirely, defer the bonsplit pane-header builder.** Concretely:

- Build `PaneTitleBarView` mounted inside `PanelContentView`, *above* the existing `SurfaceTitleBarView`, keyed by `(workspaceId, paneId)`. Render only when `PaneMetadataStore.value(for: .title, paneId:)` is non-empty. Add a new `Workspace.paneTitleBarState(paneId:)` paralleling `surfaceTitleBarState(panelId:)`. Hot-path constraint: equatable view, no environment objects in body, only re-evaluate on revision-counter change.
- Reuse the existing `titleBar_*` theme roles for background, foreground, border. Differentiate visually via opacity or weight (Decision 7's "stack reads as one nested identity, not two competing strips"), not separate color tokens. This is what Phase 3 wanted; it's free now because the theming plan already shipped.
- Layer the right-click menu and inline rename into the same PR. Small enough that splitting them adds churn without improving review. Rename writes via `PaneMetadataStore.setMetadata` with `source: .explicit`. Hide sets `paneTitleBarUserCollapsed` for the session.
- Localize all new strings at the call site; spawn a translator sub-agent for the six locales after impl lands.

**This degrades Decision 7 ("pane title above the entire pane, surface title directly below") to "pane title above the active surface's chrome."** In a multi-surface pane, the pane title appears only inside the active tab's chrome stack, sitting above the surface title bar but *below* bonsplit's tab bar. To honor strict Decision 7 (above the bonsplit tab bar, sitting above the whole pane regardless of which surface is active), bonsplit needs a `paneHeader: (PaneID) -> View` slot. That's a vendor change with its own review surface and would gate Phase 1 on a submodule bump. Given how active the chrome layer is right now (C11-6 in main, C11-10 / CMUX-10 actively writing flash overlay logic in the same files), I'd rather ship the in-surface mount, see how it reads, and only invest in the bonsplit change if the visual outcome demands it.

### Operator decisions I need

**1. Phase split: 1+2 absorbed (recommended) or strict Phase 1 only?** Recommend absorbing. The original three-phase split was sized for an in-flight theming plan that has since shipped. Phase 1 alone gives a strip you can't easily edit; Phase 1+2 gives operators a usable rename-from-keyboard surface in one PR. Trident review handles the larger surface area.

**2. Mount point: in-surface (recommended) or bonsplit pane-header builder?** Recommend in-surface. The bonsplit-builder route is correct architecturally (true Decision 7) but adds a vendor scope and a submodule push. We can revisit if the visual stack feels wrong.

**3. Decision 1, centered title?** The actual surface title bar is **left-aligned**, not centered. Recommend following the surface precedent and left-aligning the pane title. Decision 1 is stale.

**4. Decision 7, visual differentiation between pane and surface bars?** Recommend same `titleBar_*` tokens, with the pane bar at primary foreground full weight and the surface bar at secondary foreground or `0.85` opacity. Stack reads top-down as "why this pane, then what's showing in it." Confirm or override.

**5. Decision 8, bonsplit unopinionated?** Strictly true if we go in-surface. False if we add `paneHeader` to bonsplit. Recommend keeping Decision 8 as a hard rule for this PR; only add `paneHeader` if (3) and (4) deliver insufficient visual separation.

**6. Coordination with CMUX-10's flash work?** CMUX-10 is writing `flash_state` metadata plus envelope visuals to the same chrome layer right now. Recommend letting CMUX-10 land first if it's close, then mounting on top. If you'd rather this lands first, I'll coordinate via the CMUX-10 delegator. Either is fine; I default to the lower-merge-conflict path.

**7. Cohesion vs extra ticket for theming polish?** Recommend shipping in this PR with the existing tokens, no separate Phase-3 ticket. If we mint new `paneTitleBar_*` tokens later, that's a follow-up. Today's tokens are sufficient.

**8. cmux→c11 naming in the ticket text?** The description says "the in-flight c11mux theming plan" and "PLAN DOC: docs/c11mux-pane-title-bar-plan.md," both stale. Recommend I update the ticket description as part of the Plan phase. Only flagging because c11 user-facing copy should not say "c11mux."

---

Status now → `needs_human`. I'll resume on Plan once you confirm scope (here, or as a Lattice comment on CMUX-12, or in this delegator pane). Default if you say "go": all 8 recommendations stand and I move to Plan.
Operator confirmed scope ("Go"): all 8 recommendations from the scope-alignment comment stand. Moving to Plan. Status -> in_planning. Spawning Plan sub-agent in a sibling surface in pane:23.

**Plan complete.** Note at `.lattice/notes/task_01KPHEP3C31TW8SP5N245X8XHW.md`.

Phase 1+2 absorbed; mount inside `PanelContentView.swift` above `SurfaceTitleBarView` (degraded Decision 7 — in-surface, not above the bonsplit tab bar). State on `Workspace`: three pane-keyed flags (`paneTitleBarCollapsed` / `…UserCollapsed` / `…Hidden`) plus two `@Published` mirrors (`paneTitles`, `paneDescriptions`) so the view sees published changes without subscribing to `PaneMetadataStore`. Visual differentiation reuses `titleBar_foreground` (pane bar) vs `titleBar_foregroundSecondary` (surface bar when pane bar is above). No new theme roles. No bonsplit edits. Promoted `sanitizeDescriptionMarkdown` / `titleBarMarkdownTheme` to file-internal so the new view reuses them.

Commit grouping (4):
1. Workspace state + observable mirrors (no view yet, build-green foundation)
2. `PaneTitleBarView` + `PanelContentView` mount + dual-bar emphasis
3. Right-click menu (Rename / Hide / Expand-Collapse) + inline rename
4. Ticket-text + plan-doc cleanup (`c11mux-` → `c11-`, banner on Phase 1+2 absorption)

Plus a small follow-up commit 5 for the six-locale translation pass after impl lands. Test plan: behavioral seams on `Workspace.paneTitleBarState` and `setPaneTitle`; no SwiftUI snapshot or xcstrings-shape tests (per CLAUDE.md test policy). Risks: CMUX-10 file overlap on `PanelContentView.swift` — defer ordering, rebase whichever lands second. Three sub-decisions made unilaterally and called out in §9 of the note (three-flag state model, promotion of file-private helpers, banner-vs-delete on the plan doc) — please sanity-check before spawning Impl.

Stopping here. Ready for `planned` transition + Impl spawn.
